replies.author: @DocMicrowave
replies.text:
​@michaellenwatkins6846The term "blockbuster" is thrown around so much these days that it has lost its meaning.  

Back in the 80s, term was used like after the first weekend or two that a movie did amazingly well.
Now they use the term to describe a movie well before it comes out.  
I guess thinking the term will add to interest in seeing a film.
There have been a few "blockbusters" this year that ended  up far less than the term implies.

replies.author: @JacobPaul-ix7oc
replies.text:
Demoralization is the first stage of ideological subversion, and it involves the systematic undermining of a society’s moral and ethical foundations. In the 1980s, KGB defector Yuri Bezmenov explained that this process typically takes 15–20 years, roughly the time needed to educate one generation. During this stage, foreign agents seek to infiltrate educational institutions, media, and cultural platforms to influence the minds of young people. The goal is to instill a sense of disillusionment, moral relativism, and a rejection of traditional values.
In the demoralization phase, individuals are conditioned to question their own cultural identity, history, and societal norms. The emphasis is on creating a sense of instability and disorientation within the target population. This sets the stage for the subsequent phases of ideological subversion.

As the younger generation embraces new values, such as Marxism and Leninism, the older generation slowly loses control simply through attrition. Again, true facts no longer matter during this stage, but rather creating perceptions are of paramount importance.

Eliminating American exceptionalism, fundamental change of national identity, structural deconstruction of foundational principles, elimination of religion.
Embedding a new societal design upon the psyche of generations through ideological academia, peer pressure by elites upon academics and society to convince that prior values were inherently flawed, racist, prejudiced, etc.  

National identity is diluted with aspersions towards historical references.  National History is rewritten, re-defined, and molded to fit the new intended behavioral model and create the new values. 


Disney wants you to question what is good and evil, and to question if society's definition is wrong.  This is why they take established evil characters and turn them into oppressed victims of the Patriarchy or traditionalism.  
Art is social, cultural and national history and must be molded to fit Marxist ideologies, which is why "modern audience" versions of established IP are deconstructed and go "woke" (that is, Cultural Marxism).

replies.author: @ZachFazio-v3y
replies.text:
@jonfreeman9682 How did it have a good run?  It lost millions.  That's defined as a flop.  

It generated decent revenue in comparison with other entries in the franchise, but the budget was too high.  Because of that budget you needed a much bigger box office.  

The film lost $80 million at the box office.  You can't spin that into some type of success lol.  WTF?  You don't spend that much money to hopefully break even in a couple years down the road via streaming.  I mean explain to me how the film will make up $80 million on streaming?   

Gravy would be if the film actually made a profit.  It's not "gravy" when you are still trying to recoup near $100 million.

replies.author: @jonfreeman9682
replies.text: @ZachFazio-v3y  That's true if you just look at box office.  But box office isn't as important now as in the past.  Netflix could make billions if they released their films theatrically but subscription is where the money is now.  It's not good for cinema though if all movies go straight to streaming.

replies.author: @ZachFazio-v3y
@ZachFazio-v3y
replies.text:
@jonfreeman9682 So can you not explain to me how they make back nearly a $100 million on streaming?  You're giving this basic argument.  Of course streaming is important.  But do you really think $100 million worth of new subscribers are going to sign up when Predator:Badlands hits streaming?  This film is so far behind profitability.

It's crazy you think investing $150M + into a film, losing $100 million at the box office, and best case break even years down the road is a good investment.  Streaming is important but you can't bomb that badly at the box office and pretend everything is ok lol.

replies.author: @jonfreeman9682
@ZachFazio-v3y
replies.text: @jonfreeman9682 Still nothing?  So you claim box office is not important yet have no idea how Disney recoups a $100M loss from streaming.

Reverse psychology is a technique involving the assertion of a belief or behavior that is opposite to the one desired, with the expectation that this approach will encourage the subject of the persuasion to do what is actually desired.  This technique relies on the psychological phenomenon of reactance, in which a person has a negative emotional reaction to being persuaded, and thus chooses the option which is being advocated against. 
Hollywood, a propaganda arm of the US Government, has been asserting atheistic, Liberal Marxist ideologies (woke, THE MESSAGE, "modern audience") with the expectation this approach would do exactly as intended, persuade people to go the desired route of Conservative values. 
It's working.

The producers here, from what I heard, not only did not listen to a single bloody complaint made over the past 20 years, but like Veilguard, they completely destroyed the very little the original story had going for it, tried to push another woke agenda, as if snow white couldn't blare out the sun enough with all the controversy...AND THEY STILL decided to make it.

author: @shadowphoenix6237
text: Calculating the success of a movie via inflation numbers has caveats. Too many factors are involved to get a true picture of economic success due to the ever changing global economy and inflated production costs of most movies today. It also does not take into consideration the changes in home entertainment and how those changes affect the box office. So comparing gross numbers is flawed. Raw numbers are typically used to determine immediate financial success and that is why most media outlets use those. Inflation numbers are better used to determine cultural outreach and overall ticket sales. In other words....Badlands was more financially successful than any other Predator movie. However, the outreach of both 87 predator and avp is far superior. Regarding the box office, this translates to a bigger audience for those two movies and a much wider cultural influence. This still does not take into consideration of success/failure within the streaming platforms which have become more relevant in todays world. Thus leading to a near impossible outcome with comparison since most streaming platforms do not disclose the financial results of rent/sales regarding specific movie titles.
